content:
🔥 GeoSentinel | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: Real-time global movement monitoring and geopolitical awareness for analysis and decision-making.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time ship and flight tracking • Aggregation of geodata • Geographic and geopolitical awareness • Analysis and visualization tools • Darkweb search via TOR
📖 Summary: GeoSentinel is a real-time geospatial monitoring platform that aggregates ship, flight, and geodata to provide global movement awareness. It offers tools for analysis, visualization, and decision-making, including advanced features like dark web searching and AI-powered assistance.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 CasaOS | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a user-friendly, self-hosted personal cloud experience for managing applications and services.
✨ Key Features: • Simple and elegant UI • Easy app installation and management • Supports Docker applications • Extensible plugin system • Focus on user autonomy and cost reduction
📖 Summary: CasaOS is an open-source personal cloud operating system designed for ease of use and elegance. It allows users to easily manage applications and services on their own hardware, providing a self-hosted alternative to cloud services.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 youre-the-os | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: A simulation game where players experience and manage the core functions of an operating system.
✨ Key Features: • Process management • Memory management • I/O event handling • User impatience mechanic • Desktop application
📖 Summary: You're the OS! is a Python-based game where players act as a computer's operating system. The core gameplay involves managing processes, memory, and I/O events to keep the user happy and prevent system reboots. It can be played as a desktop application or a web version.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 sia | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To autonomously improve the performance of AI models or agents on specific benchmark tasks through a self-correcting and self-refining loop.
✨ Key Features: • Autonomous performance improvement for AI systems • Iterative refinement loop with meta, target, and feedback agents • Updates both harness and weights of task-specific agents • Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini)
📖 Summary: SIA is a Self-Improving AI framework designed to autonomously enhance the performance of any AI system on benchmark tasks. It achieves this through an iterative loop involving a meta-agent, a target agent, and a feedback agent, which collectively refine the target agent's capabilities.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 SkillSpector | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Scanning AI agent skills for security vulnerabilities and malicious intent before installation.
✨ Key Features: • Multi-format input (Git repos, URLs, zip files, directories, single files) • Detection of 68 vulnerability patterns across 17 categories • Two-stage analysis: static analysis and optional LLM semantic evaluation • Live vulnerability lookups with OSV.dev integration and offline fallback • Multiple output formats (Terminal, JSON, Markdown, SARIF)
📖 Summary: SkillSpector is a Python-based security scanner designed to detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks in AI agent skills before they are installed. It provides a comprehensive analysis to ensure the safety and integrity of AI agent ecosystems.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
